title: Georgia
excerpt:
Consumer Assistance in Georgia

The Georgia Consumer Assistance Program is run by the state Office of Insurance and Safety Fire Commissioner. Staff can answer questions about health insurance regulations, rights, and responsibilities. They can help you file a complaint or appeal a health plan decision. Get consumer help with a problem or question.

Medicaid in Georgia

You can apply for Medicaid in Georgia by contacting your state Medicaid agency.

Shop Health Insurance Marketplace® in Georgia

SHOP Health Insurance Marketplace® for Employees in Georgia

Health Insurance Marketplace® in Georgia

CHIP in Georgia

Georgia PeachCare for Kids covers uninsured children in families with moderate incomes that are too high to qualify for Medicaid. You can find out whether your children qualify for Georgia PeachCare for Kids right now. Fill out a Marketplace application and find out whether your children qualify. Learn more about Georgia PeachCare for Kids.
